programme.title: Deadly Parade Crash Trial
programme.desc: A dance team coach took the stand in the deadly parade crash trial and gave a harrowing account of how a red SUV hit many of the dancers in her group. They were among the sixty-one people injured. In the end, six people died.

programme.title: Deadly Parade Crash Trial
programme.desc: A dance coach breaks down remembering the moment she and her team faced the oncoming car.

programme.title: Deadly Parade Crash Trial
programme.desc: The State's main focus at this point in the deadly parade crash trial was on calling the survivors to the stand. In all, six people died when an SUV plowed into the crowd.

programme.title: Deadly Parade Crash Trial
programme.sub-title: Clocking In The Speed Of The SUV

programme.title: Wrong Apartment Murder Trial

programme.title: Wrong Apartment Murder Trial
programme.desc: Information from videos and phone records help to create a timeline of Amber Guyger's movements.

programme.title: Wrong Apartment Murder Trial
programme.desc: Jurors listen to several people who were shaken by the sound of gunshots the night Botham Jean died.

programme.title: Wrong Apartment Murder Trial
programme.desc: The medical examiner explained to the jury how the fatal bullet took Botham Jean's life.

programme.title: Sex, Cyanide And A Doctor On The Run Case
programme.desc: Scared and on the run was how Yazeed Essa's defense team described him in the weeks after his wife, Rosemarie, died. His good friend explained to jurors his communication with the Yazeed after he fled the U.S. Emotions took over Dr. Sudesh Kedar when he recalled Rosemarie's death. Yazeed's brother, Firas, returned to the stand to plead the fifth. Prosecutors alleged he committed perjury when he testified that the defendant did not confess to him that he killed his wife.

programme.title: Sex, Cyanide And A Doctor On The Run Case
programme.desc: On the stand, the fellow doctor and defendant's close friend elaborated about potassium cyanide, the poison prosecutors alleged Yazeed Essa used to kill his wife, Rosemarie, so he could start a life with one of his mistresses. Jurors then listened to what Yazeed was claiming after he left the country. His defense argued that he went to Lebanon because he was scared and embarrassed that people would learn about his double life after the death of wife, Rosemarie.

programme.title: Sex, Cyanide And A Doctor On The Run Case
programme.desc: Prosecutors accusing Yazeed Essa of murdering his wife, Rosemarie, with cyanide-laced calcium pills brought in a witness who could tell the jury about those pills. This expert broke down details about the manufacturing and bottling process. Jurors heard from Raymond Stadnick, vice president of quality control at supplement manufacturer NTBY. He told them that there were no problems on the manufacturing end with the calcium pills that his wife, Rosemarie, took.

programme.title: Sex, Cyanide And A Doctor On The Run Case
programme.desc: Prosecution had more questions for the manufacturer of the dietary supplements that Rosemarie Essa was taking at the time she died. The State put the focus on these calcium pills Rosemarie Essa had been taking before she died. They wanted to show that there was no manufacturing defect as they tried to prove that it was her husband, Yazeed, who tampered with them by lacing them with a deadly poison.

programme.title: Killer Mistake
programme.desc: Untangle the web of lies a violent sociopath wove on an online dating site.

programme.title: Killer Mistake
programme.desc: Learn why a mother’s hunch helped solve a hunter’s strange disappearance.

programme.title: #DEAD2ME
programme.desc: Consider the obsession that pushed an alcoholic to brutally kill his new friend.

programme.title: #DEAD2ME
programme.desc: Crack open the case of a catfishing killer who lured a young mum to her death.

programme.title: #DEAD2ME
programme.desc: Determine how a pair of empty nesters fell into a dangerous downward spiral.

programme.title: Hunting The Catfish Crime Gang
programme.desc: Watch a man take his life back from the ruthless gang that stole his identity.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.sub-title: The True Story of Mississippi Burning
programme.desc: In 1964, civil rights workers who set out to educate Southern Black people about the right to vote disappeared in Mississippi. After they were found lynched, the FBI gained the trust of an informant who led to the conviction of local KKK members.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: The decapitation of an African-American male led the Miami-Dade police and the FBI along a trail of murder.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1986, a psychotic killer shot and killed a police officer at point-blank range, leading investigators in a wild chase across the Midwest. As his tally of murders and abductions grew, the FBI raced against time to put a stop to the deranged gunman.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1984, a couple swept through six states like a plague. They targeted adults and young girls for kidnap and murder. The gruesome crime spree left eight people dead, so the FBI launched a manhunt for killers Alton Coleman and Debra Denise Brown.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In Washington DC, several officers had been sitting in their patrol cars when a man opened fire on them.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1985, South Carolina serial killer Larry Gene Bell was a predator, abducting girls and young women.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A loner and distrustful of authority, Claude Dallas lived off the land, poaching wildlife and selling the hides. When two game wardens confronted Dallas about his deeds, they were shot and killed.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1982, a California highway patrol officer pulled a young woman over for speeding. He bullied her into sex and then killed her. He then used a decade of police experience to try to cover up his crime and convince investigators of his innocence.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: The FBI discovered that the criminals had a hidden agenda beyond their personal wealth.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: After a young woman went missing, police suspected that her well-connected, married ex-lover was responsible.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1990, a 14-year-old girl ran away from her Texas home and disappeared; six months later, her body was found hundreds of miles away in an Illinois barn. Agents tracked down a trucker who they believed was responsible for over 50 murders.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A teenager was shot to death on a hunting ground in Virginia. His death was initially considered an accident but investigators soon suspected foul play. Years later, a man contacted the FBI with a complex story of conspiracy and murder-for-hire.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A pair of convicted felons escaped the custody of a local deputy and fled across the country in a wave of abductions and car thefts. When a foreign military officer disappeared along the trail, the FBI believed the pair were growing more violent.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A cross-country crime spree over a Labor Day weekend terrorized the nation. In a small Ohio community, an elderly woman and teenage boy disappeared within hours of one another. The police suspected the cases were linked.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: The FBI began an undercover operation to expose the Chicago Mafia's infiltration into the city's surrounding suburbs. The investigation resulted in the arrests of former police chief Michael J. Corbitt and Alan Masters for murder and racketeering.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: On October 1, 1993, a stranger abducted 12-year-old Polly Klaas during a slumber party in her home. FBI agents collected fibers from her bedroom and discovered, through the use of laser light sources, a palm print on Polly's bed.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A class field trip ended with an 11-year-old girl missing. When the parents failed to locate their daughter in their quiet Illinois town, the FBI joined the investigation, working with friends and family in an urgent search to find the missing girl.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: In January 1973, 20-year-old mother of one Dawn Magyar vanished while on a shopping trip in Owosso, Michigan.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Colin Pitchfork is a child murder who killed fifteen-year-olds Lynda Mann and Dawn Ashworth in the 1980s.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of the serial killer who brutally snatched away the lives of eight innocent women during a decade-long killing spree in Louisiana.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Wayne Adam Ford is an American serial killer who strangled and dismembered three of his four victims. The first body was found in October 1997 and had been so thoroughly dismembered that her identity remains unknown.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: William Bonin is thought to have raped, tortured and murdered a minimum of twenty-one boys and young men.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Over six years, Eddie Seda, the copycat Zodiac killer stalked the streets of New York City, in search of easy targets. His cowardly attacks culminated with the ferocious murder of a woman, who had only asked him for a cigarette.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A family's worst nightmare when 16-year-old Becky Watts goes missing from her home and is found dead a few days' later, murdered by her own stepbrother.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Catherine Nevin and her husband Tom were successful publicans and property developers in the Dublin and Wicklow areas. Catherine reported a break-in and violent attack at their pub one night in 1996, which left Tom dead.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: In March 2005, the gardaí recovered human body parts from the Royal Canal in Dublin. The victim's head was missing. He was identified as Farah Swaleh Noor, a Kenyan with a history of violence against women.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Joshua Wade had all the hall marks of a killer. We'll never understand the motives behind his random killings.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Randy Kraft was a well-spoken, well-respected computer expert...and a prolific serial killer. Convicted in 1989 of murdering 16 men, he's suspected of having taken the lives of more than 60 victims.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Steven Dean Gordon and Frank Cano murdered at least four sex workers in California between 2012 and 2013.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of the I-95 killer, who brutally killed six men in 1994 before being executed three years later.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A profile into the murderer who terrorised the gay community as the 'Trash Bag Killer' and confessed to killing 32 victims.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of the killer who took four lives during a chilling two-day killing spree in 2004 after being inspired by horror films.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Willing to kill for just $20 and drugs, Joshua Gardner commits his first murder at 24. Even behind bars, he can't contain his rage, brutally beating a fellow inmate to death.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Having committed over 27 assaults on correctional officers, Billy Tracy is one of the most violent offenders on death row.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: At 18, Christa Pike commits a brutal murder and becomes one of the youngest ever women sent to death row.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: One of the UK's most infamous serial killers, Joanna Dennehy brutally stabbed three men to death.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Seattle police had no suspects in the violent murder of post-grunge singer, Mia Zapata. More than a decade passed before the evidence could be used by forensic scientists to identify the killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The wife of a respected police officer was murdered in her own home. The crime went unsolved for more than a decade, until a newly formed cold case unit took a fresh look at the evidence.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A body is found stuffed into a suitcase forcing the police to search though the landfill for another missing victim.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2002, funeral director Lonnie Turner, Sr. was found shot to death. His son Lonnie Jr. became the prime suspect, particularly after it was discovered that the murder was committed with his gun. However, he had an alibi for the time of the murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The crime scene was especially violent: A couple had been shot to death. Was their teenage daughter complicit?

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman was brutally murdered in her home and the only witnesses to the crime were the family dogs. An expert in canine behavior was convinced the killer knew both the victim and the animals.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A brutal murder, lots of suspects and conflicting evidence but the forensics were clear on one thing: The killer knew his victim. And that alone gave investigators a head start.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In a tragic twist of fate, just days after the woman sold her home and moved to a modest trailer, a fire took both the trailer and her life. But the autopsy proved this was no accident.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a college co-ed vanished without a trace, her fellow students were concerned about her safety and their own.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Christina Sanoubane was murdered in her Cedar Rapids apartment after moving away from her abusive boyfriend.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A six-year-old girl ran and hid when she saw her grandmother being beaten to death, but the man followed, beat and assaulted her. She said the assailant was her uncle, who was convicted.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a college student is reported missing, police believe their investigation will be like countless others.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A Michigan State University grad student disappeared and was presumed dead. With the help of a professor of geological sciences, police hoped to get the "dirt" on her killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A murder trail turned cold, until police got a call from a woman whose husband, Gerald Powers, had a criminal past and a fondness for Chevy Berettas.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1987, the death of Crystal Purcell was considered an accident. Then in 2001, Barbara Purcell called police to suggest that her estranged husband had killed Crystal.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2006, Darlene VanderGiesen received threatening emails and then disappeared. Tracking the source of the emails led police to the home of Daphne Wright, where they believe a murder was committed.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2004, Rebecca Barney and her soon-to-be ex-husband Fred were found shot to death in their Tulsa, Oklahoma, home, which had been set on fire.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Foul play is suspected when Fort Worth factory worker Glenda Furch disappears after completing her shift.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2008, college co-ed Jenna Verhaalen was found dead in her Bryan, Texas apartment. Petechial hemorrhages in her eyes indicated that she was strangled.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The victim was a self-made man who never minced words. Perhaps he was murdered by a disgruntled client.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The body of an attractive young woman was found a mile from her abandoned car. Police were especially concerned when they realized the victim had come to them for protection just two weeks earlier.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When the number of bloodstains at a scene were abnormal, it was up to forensic scientists to find out why.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A lifelong resident of the tiny town of Lefroy, Tasmania was murdered outside his own home. Robbery appeared to be the motive, but with no suspects, the investigation came to a halt.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The medical examiner ruled a death an accident, but the detectives thought the evidence indicated otherwise.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A surgeon is stabbed to death, and the most likely suspect is seen in a restaurant at the time of the murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A skeleton was discovered in the North Carolina marshlands. Investigators learned she'd been dead for months.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: For twelve years, the murder of a young woman went unsolved, but with the passage of time came the development of technology. Could a used tissue found at the crime scene give police the evidence they need to bring a killer to justice?

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Tracey Frame, 35, was convicted of killing David Nixon in April 2002 after he planned to end their relationship.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2001, Ginger Hayes and her infant son Nicholas were abducted during a carjacking and the crime had been reported by a witness within minutes of occurrence.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2008, the body of Colorado real estate developer Alan Helmick was found by his wife, Miriam, who then became the prime suspect.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A young, attractive hairdresser was sexually assaulted and murdered in her own beauty salon. The evidence at the crime scene didn't match any of the suspects and the case went cold for ten years.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In the 1990s, the media dubbed Richard Rogers the "Last Call Killer" because he targeted men in gay bars.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A Russian immigrant is found dead and a strange phone call to his office is the only lead investigators have.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A wealthy man and his wife were attacked by three masked men outside their luxurious Louisiana home. He was shot dead and she was forced to open their hidden safe.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Digital enhancement of security camera video shows that what appears to be a casual encounter is actually a forced abduction, leading to murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2004, Mary Ann Clibbery was found brutally murdered in her Illinois business and investigators had to determine if this was a robbery gone wrong or a calculated murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a man's story of a killing contradicted physical evidence, investigators turned to forensic science.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A suspicious person is carrying large swords in his backpack. A traffic stop results in one passenger taking off on foot. A search warrant is executed for a grow operation. The arrest of a gang member who has drugs.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.sub-title: Just Another Day in the Life of a Deputy
programme.desc: A deputy finds an overturned vehicle with an intoxicated driver inside. A foreign national from Guatemala is arrested for driving with a suspended license. A traffic stop leads to an arrest on a felony warrant.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Police search for a robbery suspect who has a felony warrant for his arrest. Deputies respond to a call from a mother who claims her son is attacking her. A traffic stop leads to a drug possession charge.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.sub-title: Mexican Cartel Pot Grow in Our Backyard
programme.desc: Behind the scenes of the planning and execution of a large drug bust.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: We follow Deputy Bryan Payne up close and personal on his night shift as he goes about a typical day for a deputy in El Dorado County.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A deputy responds to a report of a man and woman fighting. Deputies investigate a reported verbal disturbance outside an apartment complex.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A deputy responds to a 9-1-1 call about a possible burglary. Deputies answer a domestic violence call, where a man claims his wife choked him.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: The West El Dorado Narcotics Enforcement Team plans and executes the raid of a large drug cartel. The team converges on the operation of a mid-level methamphetamine dealer.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Detectives and deputies arrest sex traffickers in a prostitution sting operation. A store owner reports a suspicious man looking into car windows. A deputy is called to a business where four people sitting in a car appear to be up to no good.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: The search of a vehicle reveals drugs and a handgun. A homeless person trashes a woman's house.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Responding to a domestic violence call, a deputy confronts an angry, non-compliant man who refuses to identify himself. Deputies try to find out who shot a family's dog. A man is passed out in a truck.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Officers respond to an ongoing fight between drug users. A deputy responds to a report of a drug overdose. A woman complains she is being harassed because she is of Mexican descent.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A deputy responds to a report of domestic abuse along with an assault with a deadly weapon. A traffic stop on a vehicle with an expired registration reveals three women who have some surprising drug habits.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A woman is stopped for driving under the influence. A mentally unbalanced young man arms himself and refuses to cooperate with law enforcement.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Deputies wishing to be part of the SWAT team face a testing procedure that is harsh and demanding.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.sub-title: Bikes for Sale: Cash, Money Order or Hash Oil Accepted
programme.desc: The West El Dorado Narcotics Enforcement Team conceive, plan and execute a ploy to catch a person suspected of offering hash oil to trade for goods on Craigslist.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: An anonymous caller gives the whereabouts of a stolen vehicle. The driver of a car stopped at an intersection is unresponsive. A deputy responds to a report of a vehicle that may have been used in an earlier crime.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A sting operation results in the recovery of over 12 ounces of meth. An alarm goes off at a coffee shop. A report of a stolen vehicle at a gas station.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Officers try to clear out several hostile transients who are camping illegally. A deputy describes a mental game he uses to keep him sharp while on patrol. A man refuses to leave a car dealership.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A deputy tries to make contact with a parolee who has a history of fighting. Officers respond to an assault at a gas station. A husband allegedly assaults his wife.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Officers respond to an ongoing fight between drug users. A deputy responds to a report of a drug overdose. A woman complains she is being harassed because she is of Mexican descent.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Deputies investigate a report of a woman throwing objects at passing cars. According to a domestic violence call, an intoxicated woman throws heavy objects at her husband.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.sub-title: Just Another Day in the Life of a Deputy
programme.desc: A deputy finds an overturned vehicle with an intoxicated driver inside. A foreign national from Guatemala is arrested for driving with a suspended license. A traffic stop leads to an arrest on a felony warrant.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.sub-title: Nine-Time Loser Goes for His Tenth!
programme.desc: Police take down a huge marijuana grow operation run by a suspect who is a nine-time felon. A report of gunfire in a neighborhood. A man accuses his girlfriend of hitting him.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A pizza shop owner tells the police that there is a crazed, naked man running around in his kitchen. A deputy is called to a home where suspected parental abuse and child abuse is being reported. Verbal disturbance over a Jeep.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A woman is stopped for driving under the influence. A mentally unbalanced young man arms himself and refuses to cooperate with law enforcement.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: The entire sheriff's department engages in an active school shooter training. A deputy sees through a string of lies after approaching a drug addict walking the streets at night. A transient verbally assaults pedestrians.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Police execute a search for heroin in a horrifically filthy house. A report of suspicious activity in a grocery store parking lot. Officers investigate an abandoned trailer.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Officers do a probation search of a known heroin user. A person with two felony warrants has a propensity to evade capture. A motorcyclist is pinned between vehicles after an accident.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A dangerous fugitive is wanted for assault and battery. A deputy stops an unregistered vehicle. A look at the daily procedures deputies must follow before going on patrol.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A deputy responds to a house fire several days before Christmas. Later, he plays Santa to some kids who lost everything in the fire.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Detectives and deputies arrest sex traffickers in a prostitution sting operation. A store owner reports a suspicious man looking into car windows. A deputy is called to a business where four people sitting in a car appear to be up to no good.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A bear is reported to be wandering near people's homes. Deputies respond to a domestic violence call.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Deputies struggle with a wanted man who had too much to drink. A deputy deals with two young thieves. A young man bleeding from the head gives an officer a difficult time.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A probationer has a warrant out for his arrest. Two people are found sleeping in a car behind a drug store.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A deputy answers a 9-1-1 call about a lady stealing from a convenience store. A woman claims a prostitute solicited her husband in a restaurant.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: The investigation into a case of domestic abuse leads to the arrest of the abuser's friend on a $25,000 warrant. A deputy investigates two women who are parked behind a closed business late at night.

programme.sub-title: Fire Is Coming, Time to Run!

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Police attempt to arrest a drug dealer. A young woman is found lying on the side of the road. Officers try to catch people who are willing to buy alcohol for minors.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A traffic stop reveals a man with stolen food and burglary tools who drives without a license. A deputy responds to a call about two intoxicated men. Two men are reportedly fighting at a department store.
